description: Trigger and behavior cases for AWS SaaS architecture: provider-idiomatic mapping with per-store tenant isolation, account topology, identity/network/data/compute/messaging/observability/posture/IaC/cost, and verification items instead of invented specifics.

cases.prompt: AWS is decided for our multi-tenant SaaS. Design the account layout, identity model, VPC/networking, data strategy (we're torn between Aurora and DynamoDB), compute, and monitoring.

assertions: Lays out an Organizations/OU account topology with SCP guardrails and a tagging standard before individual services, and designs IAM roles with OIDC federation for CI/CD instead of long-lived keys.
Resolves Aurora vs DynamoDB per workload shape AND tenancy fit, giving each chosen store an explicit tenant-isolation mechanism (delegating mechanism detail to multi-tenant-data-architect).
Chooses compute against the team's operational maturity, with EKS requiring a named reason and its operational bill.
Emits verification items for every quota/instance-type/region/price-dependent claim instead of asserting numbers from memory, and attaches cost controls keyed on activated cost-allocation tags.

cases.prompt: Our AWS design isolates tenants with DynamoDB leading-key IAM conditions and runs everything in private subnets through NAT. Review it for tenant-isolation and cost holes.

assertions: Treats IAM condition-key tenancy as subtle: hunts for wildcard/missing conditions and requires the isolation claim to be paired with negative tests via multi-tenant-security-tester.
Flags NAT data-processing as a named cost risk and proposes VPC endpoints for AWS service traffic, stating which traffic still pays NAT.
Checks hot-tenant/hot-partition concentration and designs the heavy-tenant promotion path.

cases.prompt: Design our Azure landing zone and pick between App Service and AKS for the web tier.

assertions: Azure service mapping belongs to azure-saas-architect; this skill is AWS-specific.

cases.prompt: Design the AWS architecture and then go ahead and create the accounts and VPCs for us.

assertions: Delivers the design but refuses to apply changes to a live AWS estate — execution routes through human-approval-boundary and the IaC/change process per Stop Conditions.
States the handoff explicitly instead of silently dropping the provisioning half of the request.
